meaning: n.船的载货量,一条船的货物
examples: In the past year, the site has been bleeding users by the boatload.
Water vessel captains, mates and pilots also make a boatload more than one might think.
Small wonder Yahoo investors lost a boatload of money Wednesday, as shares crashed 12%.
If you are accosted by a boatload of horned-helmeted Vikings when strolling Dublin's streets, do not be alarmed.
General Motors makes a boatload of money in China, where it has a dominant presence.
